response:
Emily gathered 24 blueberries. David collected 15 more than Emily which means he had 39 blueberries. Additionally David collected three times as many blueberries as Sophia. This means Sophia collected 13 blueberries. Together they had 24 plus 39 plus 13 blueberries. The total number of blueberries they collected was 76.

***

They used all the blueberries to make muffins. Each batch of muffins needed 9 blueberries. To find out how many batches they could make they divided the total number of blueberries by 9. The result was 8 batches with 4 blueberries left over. They sold each batch for $5 so they earned 8 times 5 dollars. The total amount they earned from selling the muffins was $40.

***

The leftover blueberries were not used for making muffins. They had 4 blueberries that could not be used for another batch. These were not sold and remained with them. The total money earned from the muffins was $40. This was the amount they made from the blueberries they collected.

***

They used all the blueberries they had to make muffins. The money they earned from selling the muffins was $40. This was the amount they made from the blueberries they collected. The youth who collected the blueberries made a total of $40 from their efforts.
